name: Crispz - Z-Image upscaler + detailer
description: ESRGAN upscale + Z-Image Turbo refinement pass (img2img BF16). External process, invoked via CLI.
license_note: Models pulled separately (HuggingFace for Z-Image, local ESRGAN folder).

runtime.note_isolation: crispz runs in ITS OWN environment (torch 2.7 / cu128 + diffusers from source). NEVER import it into the Fooocus process.

server.comment: Optional persistent mode (stage 3). Keeps the model warm across calls and frees VRAM after idle_timeout. Prefer this when chaining many upscales. Requires fastapi + uvicorn.

endpoints.upscale: POST /upscale (JSON: input path + any setting incl. preset) -> {output, size, esrgan_s, refine_s, total_s}

server.measured: RTX 5090, 2K: first call ~66s (cold, model load), warm calls ~46s. The per-call model load is the main source of uneven CLI timings.

env.comment: Two strategies, host's choice. Default = fresh_venv. crispz needs torch 2.7 cu128 which Fooocus does not have, hence the explicit torch step.

fresh_venv.description: Create a fresh venv and install torch cu128 then the deps.

steps.name: pip upgrade
steps.warn: Required: the fresh venv ships an old pip that rejects typing_extensions from the PyTorch index (name normalization bug -> ResolutionImpossible).

reuse_python.description: Reuse an interpreter that already has torch 2.7 cu128 (the machine's system py -3.10), via --system-site-packages.
reuse_python.base_python_hint: Path to be provided by the user at install time (e.g. py -3.10).

output.parse: Each stdout line is an absolute path to an output image.

params.label: ESRGAN model
params.choices_parse: one file name per line on stdout

params.label: Use case
params.note: Auto settings for a scenario. Explicit flags override the preset.

params.label: Diffusion tile (4K+)
params.note: 0 = whole image. >0 tiles the Z-Image pass: caps VRAM, enables 4K+. Try 1024-1280.

params.label: CPU offload (diffusion VRAM)
params.note: Lowers the diffusion VRAM peak. 'sequential' (~9 GB) lets crispz coexist with a loaded host model.

host_hints.reason: crispz loads ESRGAN + Z-Image BF16. On a shared GPU, offloading the Fooocus SDXL model before the call avoids OOM.

vram_measured.pipeline_4k_whole: OOM expected in whole-image mode (2K already saturates the card)

pipeline_4k_tiled.note: Diffusion tiling: VRAM bounded by tile size, independent of final resolution. No OOM.

vram_measured.note: Re-measured on an idle card (1.2 GB baseline): same numbers -> crispz's real footprint, not contamination. Without offload the 2K reserved peak (32.35) > physical VRAM (31.8 GiB) = spill into Windows shared memory (WDDM), slow.

host_hints.vram_note: Without offload, the 2K pipeline saturates a 32 GB card. Two knobs (both implemented) fix this: (1) --cpu-offload: 'model' ~24 GB reserved, 'sequential' ~9 GB with little speed loss; (2) --refine-tile (diffusion tiling): caps the VRAM peak by tile size regardless of final resolution and enables 4K+ (e.g. tile 1024 -> ~23 GB at 4K, no OOM). Recommended integration: --cpu-offload sequential for 2K, add --refine-tile 1024 for 4K. Offloading the host model before the call remains the safest belt-and-suspenders option.
